title: How to set the port of "Quick connections"？（v1.77.1）
author: bytefish163
body_snippet:
`mRemoteNG version: 1.77.1.27654. `
`Operating System and version:  20H2 of Windows 10 professional.`

I set the port number in the “Default connection property” to 22222, In quick connections, type 192.168.1.222, but it didn't work! 
Error infomation:  Connection to "192.168.1.222" via "SSH2" established by user "hp_g4" (Description: ""; User Field: "")
[mRemoteNG’s documentation ](https://mremoteng.readthedocs.io/en/latest/user_interface/default_connection_properties.html#default-connecti...

iterations.description: The Quick Connect feature supports specifying a custom port via the connection string (e.g., host:port), but the documentation currently states that only default ports are used, leading to user confusion.

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: Thanks, yes its a bug will have a look.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The Quick Connect feature supports specifying a custom port via the connection string (e.g., host:port), but the documentation currently states that only default ports are used, leading to user confusion.
Approach: Update the documentation file 'mRemoteNGDocumentation\user_interface\quick_connect.rst' to explicitly mention the syntax for specifying custom ports in the Quick Connect input field.
